items.title: SoftBank Plans Biggest US IPO Since Arm Went Public: Taps Top Wall Street Banks For A Potential $2 Billion Offering
items.description: Japan's Softbank Group Corp. (OTC: SFTBY) is reportedly in talks with major U.S. investment banks regarding an initial public offering for its payments platform, PayPay.

items.title: This unprecedented shift in unemployment suggests AI could strand white-collar knowledge workers in a jobless recovery after the next recession
items.description: "A much larger unemployment risk and anemic recovery prospects for these workers might cause the next labor market downturn to look pretty dismal."

items.title: How banks from Goldman to JPMorgan are defending against private equity poaching of junior bankers
items.description: From termination threats to quarterly check-ins, we break down how different banks are pushing back against PE's aggressive recruiting tactics.

items.title: Work-Life Balance Is Out, Hardcore Culture Is In: How Experts Suggest You Adjust To The New Era Of Work
items.description: For many, the era of work-life balance is coming to a close. “The spillover from home life to work life is not of concern to bosses these days,” Yale management professor Jeffrey Sonnenfeld told Business Insider recently. "It’s not the time to talk about what makes your creature comforts so different from everybody else’s, that somehow the company has to accommodate your needs.” Within the last year, major companies like Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) enacted return-to-office
